name: Mexican Potato Skins
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Sweet red pepper, finely Diced
1 1/2 cups Fresh corn cut from cob
recipeInstructions:
1. Scrub potatoes; prick each potato several times with a fork.
2. Bake at 400 degrees for 1 hour or until potatoes are done.
3. Saute red pepper and green onions in margarine in a large skillet over medium-high heat for 3 minutes or until tender.
4. Stir in corn, chili powder, and salt; saute for 3 minutes or until tender.
5. Remove from heat, let cool.
6. Stir in sour cream and minced cilantro. Set aside.
7. Allow potatoes to cool to touch. Cut potatoes in half lengthwise.
8. Carefully scoop out pulp, leaving about 1/8-inch-thick shells. Cut each shell in half lengthwise.
9. Pour oil to a depth of 2 to 3 inches in a Dutch oven.
10. Fry shells in hot oil (375°F) for 1 to 2 minutes or until browned.
11. Invert and drain on paper towels.
12. Place shells, skin side down, on an ungreased baking sheet.
13. Spoon corn mixture evenly into shells; sprinkle evenly with cheese.
14. Broil 5-1/2 inches from heat (with electric oven door partially opened) for 2 to 3 minutes or until cheese melts.
15. Garnish with cilantro sprigs, if desired. Serve immediately.
